title: Functional backend error when bitwidths too wide
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.53+39 (git sha1 d8b27d41c, c++ 16.0.0 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

macOS

### Reproduction Steps

Use the file: 
```sv
(* use_dsp = "yes" *) module top(
	input  [17:0] a,
	input  [17:0] b,
	input  [17:0] c,
	input  [17:0] d,
	output [17:0] out,
	input clk);

	logic  [35:0] stage0;
	logic  [35:0] stage1;

	always @(posedge clk) begin
	stage0 <= ((d + a) * b) + c;
	stage1 <= stage0;
	end

	assign out = stage1;
endmodule
```

Run the command:
```
 yosys -q p 'read_verilog -sv file.sv; hierarchy -top top; prep; clk2fflogic; write_functional_rosette -provides'
```



### Expected Behavior

No error, converts to Rosette

### Actual Behavior

ERROR: The design contains an undriven signal stage1. This is not supported by the functional backend. Call setundef with appropriate options to avoid this error. 

Note that shrinking the  registers to the correct size fixes the issue. However, I wouldn't expect this to be something that would cause a failure. Is there a way around this?
code_blocks: yosys -q p 'read_verilog -sv file.sv; hierarchy -top top; prep; clk2fflogic; write_functional_rosette -provides'
